SEARCH
Package com.societegenerale.failover.dashboard.metrics.source.sharedstore

Interface SnapshotStore

All Known Implementing Classes:
SnapshotStoreInmemory, SnapshotStoreJdbc
public interface SnapshotStore
Where pushed per-instance ClusterSnapshots are held for the shared-store tier. The default SnapshotStoreInmemory keeps the latest snapshot per instance in memory; a @ConditionalOnMissingBean lets a consumer supply a durable (JDBC) or distributed (Redis/Hazelcast) implementation without touching the source or UI.

Consistency over durability (design §5.3): exactly one snapshot per instance (latest wins on upsert, so a duplicate push never double-counts), and live() returns only snapshots fresh within the liveness window — stale peers are dropped from the aggregate, never silently summed.

Author:
Anand Manissery

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    List<MetricsSummary>
    live()
    The freshest snapshot per instance whose age is within the liveness window.
    int
    liveCount()
    Number of instances currently reporting (live within the liveness window).
    List<InstanceMetrics>
    liveInstances()
    The live snapshots as per-instance entries (id + last-seen + that instance's summary), for the dashboard's Instances view.
    long
    newestEpochMs()
    Epoch-millis of the most recent live snapshot, or 0 when none are live.
    void
    upsert(ClusterSnapshot snapshot)
    Records (or replaces) the latest snapshot for the snapshot's instance, stamping the current receive time.

  • Method Details

    • upsert

      void upsert(ClusterSnapshot snapshot)
      Records (or replaces) the latest snapshot for the snapshot's instance, stamping the current receive time.

    • live

      List<MetricsSummary> live()
      The freshest snapshot per instance whose age is within the liveness window.

    • liveInstances

      List<InstanceMetrics> liveInstances()
      The live snapshots as per-instance entries (id + last-seen + that instance's summary), for the dashboard's Instances view. Same liveness filtering as live().
      Returns:
      one InstanceMetrics per live instance

    • liveCount

      int liveCount()
      Number of instances currently reporting (live within the liveness window).

    • newestEpochMs

      long newestEpochMs()
      Epoch-millis of the most recent live snapshot, or 0 when none are live.
